title: Eriolepis ferox
taxonomy: Eriolepis ferox is classified within the plant family Polypodiaceae, commonly known as the polypody fern family. This family is part of the larger group known as Polypodiales, which includes various species of ferns. Eriolepis ferox is a type of epiphytic fern, meaning it grows on other surfaces such as trees. It belongs to the genus Eriolepis, which is a relatively small group of ferns. The classification of Eriolepis ferox reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.
uses: Eriolepis ferox, also known as the saw-leaved golden sedge, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. Its roots and rhizomes are said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been utilized to treat wounds and skin conditions. In some indigenous communities, the plant has been used to treat fever and other health issues. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness for these purposes. Traditional uses of Eriolepis ferox should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a medical professional.
